desc: Working from PMO in Ottawa before world tour to China, Qatar, and Davos. (Canada)
Visit to China to 'reset' relations; meetings with President Xi Jinping, Premier Li Qiang and senior officials on trade and security. (China)[web:116][web:244]
Bilateral visit to Qatar; meetings with Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, PM and deputy PM; focus on AI, infrastructure, energy, defence and investment. (Qatar)[web:148][web:220]
Attends World Economic Forum Annual Meeting; delivers key speech and courts investment. (Switzerland)[web:116][web:244]
Back in Ottawa after China–Qatar–Davos world tour to brief cabinet and continue domestic work. (Canada)[web:194]
Begins Indo-Pacific tour; meetings with business leaders on trade, clean energy, AI and investment. (India)[web:193][web:196]
Meets Prime Minister Narendra Modi; discussions on CEPA, defence, clean energy, tech, talent mobility. (India)[web:193][web:215]
First stop in Australia; meetings focused on trade and critical minerals. (Australia)[web:193][web:219]
Meets PM Anthony Albanese; addresses Parliament; talks on defence, maritime security, critical minerals, advanced tech. (Australia)[web:193][web:219]
Meets Japanese PM; cooperation on clean energy, advanced manufacturing, critical minerals, food and regional security. (Japan)[web:193][web:217]
Returns to Canada after India–Australia–Japan Indo-Pacific tour. (Canada)[web:122][web:241]
Stop in Yellowknife en route to Norway; focus on Arctic defence and northern communities. (Canada)[web:160][web:202]
Observes NATO exercises with Norwegian and allied leaders; focus on Arctic readiness and defence. (Norway)[web:160][web:202]
Meets Nordic leaders in Oslo to deepen trade, clean energy, innovation and Arctic co‑operation; bilateral with PM Jonas Gahr Støre. (Norway)[web:109][web:202]

Meets UK PM Sir Keir Starmer at 10 Downing Street; discussion of Iran’s attacks, Ukraine, defence production and deepening Canada–UK economic and security partnership. (United Kingdom)[web:222][web:226][web:225][web:239]

Returns to Ottawa after Norway–UK trip; as of March 21, no further foreign trips have been formally scheduled with dates beyond the India–Australia–Japan and Norway–UK tours already completed. (Canada)[web:194][web:241]
